domain: Crystal twinning (mineralogy) — reflective contact twin: two crystals of same mineral share a composition plane and are related by a non-symmetry mirror reflection across it. Plagioclase Albite Law, Carlsbad Law, Baveno Law each define specific composition planes.
specific_mechanism: Two adjacent identical-lattice crystals are joined at a shared composition plane and reflect each other across a NON-symmetry mirror plane; the twin operation is not a normal symmetry of the single crystal, yet the joined structure is mechanically stable.
llm_application: TWIN-PLANE-HEAD: in a transformer with H heads per layer, designate K head pairs (A_i, B_i) and assign each pair a fixed random orthonormal direction n_i (the composition-plane normal). Tie the weight matrices via Householder reflection: W_{B_i} = (I - 2 n_i n_i^T) W_{A_i}. Train only W_{A_i}; W_{B_i} is computed at every forward+backward step as the deterministic mirror. The K-th twinned pair therefore spans only a 2-D subspace per head-pair but contributes mirror coverage of the orthogonal complement of n_i. Cost: K free heads instead of 2K, half parameter footprint per twinned pair. Differs from (a) parameter tying like ALBERT (cross-layer IDENTITY tie not REFLECTION), (b) GQA (key sharing not mirror), (c) sign-flip BinaryConnect (sign flip not householder reflection about chosen plane) by using NON-IDENTITY householder mirror reflection across a chosen non-symmetry plane per pair.
novelty_claim: ALBERT cross-layer identity tie. GQA key sharing. BinaryConnect sign-flip. TWIN-PLANE-HEAD recombines (a) HEAD-PAIR tying + (b) HOUSEHOLDER MIRROR REFLECTION + (c) FIXED random non-symmetry plane. Three known components combined; not a wholly new artefact.
